behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic over template text (pattern/rule matching). No real filesystem, subprocess, network, or model output is required to reproduce leakage detection failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with template strings that include copied phase instructions and assert rejection; include near-miss strings to prevent overblocking.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher levels rejected: behavior is explicitly a static lint check, so no module handoff or real infra is required.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundaries (template + interpolation + fixture injection + assembly). A unit test of one formatter can miss leakage introduced during composition.
behaviors.test_description: Run real prompt assembly pipeline with representative fixtures and assert final prompt text excludes phase-instruction fragments copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System rejected: no real network/subprocess/OS-dependent behavior is required; Agentic rejected: no dependence on model judgment.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: The core claim is causal: behavior should come from loaded skill context, not embedded instructions. A stubbed model can pass regardless of context quality, hiding real regressions in skill-driven behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real test path that loads plugin/skill files, execute a live model call with a user-style prompt, and assert outcome reflects skill-context-driven behavior while prompt text remains free of hard-coded phase rules.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration rejected by behavior text 'validates behavior from that context' because correctness depends on real model response to context.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: This is harness orchestration and file-variant wiring (context source swap while prompt text stays fixed). It is primarily module and filesystem handoff, not model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute harness twice (baseline skill, degraded/missing skill variant) using identical prompt text and verify configuration/context source changes without prompt mutation.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic rejected: behavior only requires proving invariant prompt text across skill variants, not judging model output quality.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on how the real model responds to changed skill context under identical prompt text. Mocked outputs would mask regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model behavioral test with baseline skill and degraded skill section removed; keep prompt identical and assert degraded variant fails structural/behavioral criteria.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration rejected by concrete behavior text 'behavioral test fails' after SKILL.md degradation, which depends on real model decisions.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Risk is pipeline ordering across modules (model client -> artifact writer -> parser). This can be caught with integrated harness flow and deterministic model stub output.
behaviors.test_description: Instrument the harness run to assert raw response artifact is written first (timestamp/order or call-sequence check), then parser/extractor runs; verify artifact contains unmodified raw text.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic rejected: ordering/capture correctness does not depend on model semantic quality; System rejected: no external OS/network behavior is essential beyond local process/file I/O.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Need to verify assertion layer as wired in real test flow (extraction + schema/path checks), where keyword-only logic could accidentally still be used.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative outputs through the harness assertion stack, including cases with correct keywords but wrong field placement; assert structural checks fail those cases.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit rejected because single-validator tests can miss harness-level fallback to keyword checks; Agentic rejected because this behavior targets assertion mechanics, not model quality.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live model execution and regression detection based on actual model outputs; stubs cannot represent real variation where keywords are present but structure is wrong.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model prompts on baseline and regression-prone conditions; compare keyword-only pass vs structural assertion fail cases and assert structural checks detect the regression.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration rejected by explicit behavior text 'Under live model execution'; Workflow rejected because this is a single model-evaluation step, not multi-agentic pipeline chaining.
